debug_info.goal:
μ ν : Measure ℝ
inst✝¹ : IsProbabilityMeasure μ
inst✝ : IsProbabilityMeasure ν
n : ℕ
i j : Fin n
h : i ≠ j
⊢ HasLaw (fun ω => wignerMatrixMap n ω i j) μ (WignerMeasure μ ν n)

verification_message: All 1 attempts failed (1 strategies x 1 attempts each)
proof_attempts:
have h₁ : i < j ∨ j < i := by
    cases' lt_or_gt_of_ne h with h₂ h₂
    · exact Or.inl h₂
    · exact Or.inr h₂
  
  have h₂ : HasLaw (fun ω ↦ (wignerMatrixMap n ω) i j) μ (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := by
    -- Use the fact that either i < j or j < i to prove the has law property
    cases h₁ with
    | inl h₁ =>
      -- Case: i < j
      -- Use the offDiagonalHasLaw' lemma to get the result directly
      exact offDiagonalHasLaw' n i j ⟨h₁⟩
    | inr h₁ =>
      -- Case: j < i
      -- Use the symmetry of the Wigner matrix to reduce to the i < j case
      have h₃ : HasLaw (fun ω => (wignerMatrixMap n ω) j i) μ (WignerMeasure μ ν n) := offDiagonalHasLaw' n j i ⟨h₁⟩
      have h₄ : (fun ω => (wignerMatrixMap n ω) i j) = (fun ω => (wignerMatrixMap n ω) j i) := by
        funext ω
        rw [show (wignerMatrixMap n ω) i j = (wignerMatrixMap n ω) j i by
          -- Use the symmetry property of the Wigner matrix
          have h₅ := wignerMatrixSymmetric n i j ω
          simp [h₅]
        ]
      rw [h₄]
      exact h₃
  
  exact h₂
